documentation.readme: Notice kube-hunter is not under active development anymore. If you're interested in scanning Kubernetes clusters for known vulnerabilities, we recommend using Trivy. Specifically, Trivy's Kubernetes misconfiguration scanning and KBOM vulnerability scanning. Learn more in the Trivy Docs.
usage_sections: When runing with --pod flag, kube-hunter uses the service account token mounted inside the pod to authenticate to services it finds during the hunt.
if specified, --service-account-token flag takes priority when running as a pod
http (to configure, set the following environment variables:)

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with aquasecurity/kube-bench (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with aquasecurity/trivy (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the frameworks category. Fits when the engagement needs: Notice kube-hunter is not under active development anymore. If you're interested in scanning Kubernetes clusters for known vulnerabilities, we recommend using Trivy. Specifically, Trivy's Kubernetes misconfiguration scan.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Notice kube-hunter is not under active development anymore. If you're interested in scanning Kubernetes clusters for known vulnerabilities, we recommend using Trivy. Specifically, Trivy's Kubernetes misconfiguration scanning and KBOM vulnerability scanning. Learn more in the Trivy Docs.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip install kube-hunter; git clone https://github.com/aquasecurity/kube-hunter.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `kube-hunter --active --service-account-token=$KFIOSA_SECRET`. Further invocations from upstream: `kube-hunter --custom <HunterName1> <HunterName2>`; `kube-hunter --active --list --raw-hunter-names`. Notice kube-hunter is not under active development anymore. If you're interested in scanning Kubernetes clusters for known vulnerabilities, we recommend using Trivy. Specifically, Trivy's Kubernetes misconfiguration scanning and KBOM vulnerability scanning. Learn more in the Trivy Docs.

name: kube-hunter
summary: Notice kube-hunter is not under active development anymore. If you're interested in scanning Kubernetes clusters for known vulnerabilities, we recommend using Trivy. Specifically, Trivy's Kubernetes misconfiguration scanning and KBOM vulnerability scanning. Learn more in the Trivy Docs.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need frameworks, use kube-hunter because Notice kube-hunter is not under active development anymore. If you're interested in scanning Kubernetes clusters for known vulnerabilities, we recommend using T.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `kube-hunter --active --service-account-token=$KFIOSA_SECRET` as documented upstream for kube-hunter.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `kube-hunter --custom <HunterName1> <HunterName2>` as documented upstream for kube-hunter.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `kube-hunter --active --list --raw-hunter-names` as documented upstream for kube-hunter.
When chaining frameworks tools, pair kube-hunter with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `kube-hunter --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
